definition:
1. that which one needs must pay, an obligation, debt , (Odyssey by Homer); a debt for stolen cattle , (Iliad by Homer); χρεῖος ἀποστήσασθαι to pay a debt  in full, (Iliad by Homer); ἀρᾶς τίνει χρ. pays the debt demanded by  the curse, (Aeschulus Tragicus); χρέος πόλει προσάπτειν to attach a further debt , i.e. guilt  to the city, (Sophocles Tragicus); χρέος ἀποδιδόναι to repay a debt , (Herdotus Historicus):—;in plural debts , χρειῶν λύσις (Hesiod); τὴν οὐσίαν ἅπασαν χρέα κατέλιπε left all the property in outstanding debts , (Demosthenes Orator) 
2. a needful business, an affair, matter , ἑὸν αὐτοῦ χρεῖος (Odyssey by Homer): a requirement, a purpose , (Sophocles Tragicus): with genitive, like χάριν, for the sake of , σὸν οὐκ ἔλασσον ἢ κείνης χρέος (Euripides) 
3. like χρῆμα, a thing , τί χρέο; = τί χρῆμ; wherefore?  (Aeschulus Tragicus); ἐφ᾽ ὅ τι χρ. ἐμόλετ; (Euripides) 
4. in (Odyssey by Homer), ἦλθον Τειρεσίαο κατὰ χρέος seems to be = Τειρεσίᾳ χρησόμενος, I came to consult  him:—;but, κατὰ χρέος according to what is due , as is meet , (Homeric Hymns) 
5. a duty, task, charge, office , (Pindar) (ML)
